issue_title: Default to anti gravity models and not opus bridge
problem_statement: The project currently defaults to using the Opus bridge MCP tool for its model/provider configuration. The desired behavior is to default to the built-in anti gravity models instead. The most likely place this default is configured is in the anti-gravity config file and potentially in the environment example file.
implementation_plan: Inspect `.antigravity/config.yml` to identify where the default model/provider is set (likely a model or provider field referencing 'opus bridge' or an MCP tool configuration).
Update the default model/provider value in `.antigravity/config.yml` to reference the built-in anti gravity models instead of the Opus bridge MCP tool.
Inspect `.env.example` for any environment variables that configure the default model or provider and update them to reflect the new anti gravity model defaults if applicable.
Verify that no scripts in `scripts/` hardcode the Opus bridge as a default (review `scripts/run-pipeline.sh`, `scripts/forge-create.sh`, and `scripts/start-irontech.sh` for any model references).
acceptance_criteria: The default model/provider in `.antigravity/config.yml` references the built-in anti gravity models, not the Opus bridge MCP tool.
`.env.example` reflects the updated default if it contains model/provider configuration.
Running the pipeline without explicit model overrides uses the anti gravity models by default.
Opus bridge remains available as a non-default option (no removal of the capability, just a default change).
